text.title: i18n Core Principles: ICU, Keys, Plurals, Never-Concat
text.summary: The foundational rules for internationalisation: ICU MessageFormat, stable key naming, locale priority chain, and the never-concatenate rule.

rule.statement: Never compose translated text from multiple fragments; one message = one complete sentence with ICU placeholders.
rule.rationale: Word order differs across languages. "Hello, " + name + "!" becomes "name-san, konnichiwa" in Japanese — fragment concatenation produces grammatically invalid output in most non-English locales.

example.code: # WRONG - breaks Japanese, Arabic, German word order msg = _("Hello, ") + user.name + _("!")
example.note: Fragments cannot be reordered by translators.

example.code: # CORRECT - single ICU message with placeholder msg = _("Hello, {name}!").format(name=user.name) # or via babel ngettext for plurals: msg = _n("{count} item", "{count} items", count).format(count=count)

rule.statement: Use ICU MessageFormat for plurals (plural selector) and gender/context (select); never write if n == 1 ... else.
rule.rationale: English has 2 plural forms; Russian/Ukrainian 3; Arabic 6; Welsh 6; Irish 5. if/else breaks for any language beyond English.

rule.statement: Use hierarchical stable keys (checkout.button.submit), never English text as the key.
rule.rationale: When copy is edited, text-as-key breaks all translation lookups in every locale. Hierarchical keys remain stable through copy changes.

rule.statement: Resolve locale in this order: explicit user setting > account default > Accept-Language > geo-IP > app default. Persist user choice in DB.
rule.rationale: Accept-Language alone changes per device on multilingual systems. Persisting user choice ensures consistent experience across sessions and devices.

rule.statement: Format dates, numbers, and currencies through Intl.* (JS) or babel.numbers / babel.dates (Python) — never strftime or string-concat currency symbols.
rule.rationale: 1,234.56 (en-US) vs 1.234,56 (de-DE) vs 1 234,56 (fr-FR). MM/DD/YYYY is local to the US. $amount is wrong for anyone outside USD.

antipattern.description: Translating technical strings (API errors, log messages).
antipattern.reason: Log aggregation tools and support engineers need English. Translate only user-visible UI.
